expectations.text: Concludes that in-band chat approval is NOT safe for a wire transfer
expectations.evidence: 'No, not as described' — a chat yes is a gesture, not a safe control for irreversible wires.

expectations.text: Explains the agent can read/forge/manipulate the in-band approval (channel not isolated from the agent)
expectations.evidence: Prompt-injection can forge/coerce the approval; approval lives in the same trust boundary as the proposer.

expectations.text: Recommends out-of-band approval requiring a separate authentication factor
expectations.evidence: Out-of-band approvals UI / push / signed link, with step-up re-authentication (MFA/passkey/biometric) at approval time.

expectations.text: Binds the approval to the specific transaction (amount and/or recipient)
expectations.evidence: Immutable approval record (payee, IBAN, amount, currency, date); single-use, time-bound, tamper-evident; execution must match exactly.

expectations.text: Classifies wire transfer at the highest oversight tier (strong approval / T3) or excludes it above a cap
expectations.evidence: Hard caps that simply refuse regardless of approval; dual control / four-eyes above a threshold.
